title: service-accounts — subprocessors
description: Zero primary subprocessors. This module operates entirely in-process for the credential-storage + JWT-signing paths. Optional secondary subprocessors are: (a) AWS EventBridge for kid-rotation notifications (Wave 1c — when RS256 asymmetric keys move to AWS KMS-hosted material), (b) PagerDuty for on-call escalation of suspicious-activity notifications (composed via the platform-user module's routing, not directly by this module). None are engaged in the Wave 1a HS256 shipping path.
not_a_subprocessor: bcrypt — in-process libsodium, no data leaves the app
firebase/php-jwt — in-process library invoked via auth::JwtSigner; no external calls
OpenTelemetry / metrics collector — receives aggregate metrics only (no service_account_id in metric labels per metrics.json)
Segment / product analytics — receives service_account_id (internal-workforce plane events) but these are internal Stackra operational telemetry, not tenant data — same tier as platform-user events
Email delivery (SES / Postmark / SMTP relay) — handled by `notifications` module; service-accounts does not maintain its own delivery pipeline
Slack — same; delivered via notifications module for the SA-lifecycle notifications listed in notifications.json

future_subprocessors_wave_1c.name: AWS KMS
future_subprocessors_wave_1c.purpose: When Wave 1c introduces RS256 asymmetric JWT signing alongside HS256, the RS256 PRIVATE keys will be KMS-wrapped at rest (via envelope encryption). HS256 material stays in `auth::jwt_signing_keys.secret_material` under Doppler-backed encryption. This module inherits KMS as a subprocessor at that point.
